resources.string: No alarms set.\nTap + to create one.
Start alarm up to %d min early if you\'re in light sleep
Disabled. Alarm fires exactly on time.
Sound and light take %d min to reach full intensity
WakeIQ needs a few permissions to wake you reliably. Without the required ones, your alarm may not ring.
One or more required permissions are missing. Your alarm may not ring.
Without this, the alarm cannot display on your screen or make sound while the app is in the background. Required on Android 13 and above.
Without this, your alarm may fire several minutes late. Required on Android 12.
Without this, the alarm screen will not appear over your lock screen when the phone is sleeping. Required on Android 14 and above.
Android can kill WakeIQ to save battery before your alarm fires. Exempting the app from battery optimisation prevents this.
Without this, your alarm will be silenced if your phone is in Do Not Disturb mode when it fires. Tap Grant and enable it under the Alarm channel.
Without this permission, your alarm may fire several minutes late. Tap \"Allow\" to open Settings and enable exact alarms for WakeIQ.
Shows a persistent notification while your alarm is active. Without it, the alarm cannot display on your screen.
Needed to play audio files you choose from your device. Without it, only bundled sounds are available.
Allows the alarm to gradually brighten your screen. Without it, the brightness ramp feature is disabled.
Re-registers your alarms after the device reboots. Without it, alarms are lost when you restart your phone.
Tap to send an anonymous crash report and help fix this bug.

Most alarm apps yank you out of deep sleep, causing grogginess. WakeIQ is different.

Human sleep moves through cycles of light (N1/N2), deep (N3/slow-wave), and REM stages roughly every 90 minutes. Waking during deep sleep causes sleep inertia (that heavy, foggy feeling).

Smart wake monitors your phone's accelerometer during a window before your alarm. More movement = lighter sleep stage. If light sleep is detected early, the alarm triggers then so you surface naturally. If no light sleep is detected, the alarm fires at your set time as normal.

Configure the window duration in Settings.

Your phone's accelerometer detects movement during sleep. More movement = lighter sleep stage.

Low: requires significant movement to trigger early wake.
Medium: recommended for most people.
High: triggers on subtle shifts, best for light sleepers.
Warm tint within app, 6 pm – 6 am

Blue light from screens suppresses melatonin, the hormone that tells your body it\'s time to sleep. Reducing blue light in the evenings helps you fall asleep faster and improves sleep quality.\n\nThis filter only affects WakeIQ, not your device\'s global display.

Human sleep moves through cycles of light (N1/N2), deep slow-wave (N3), and REM stages every ~90 minutes. Waking during deep sleep causes sleep inertia (that heavy, foggy feeling that can last hours).\n\nWakeIQ uses your phone\'s accelerometer to detect movement patterns during sleep. More movement = lighter sleep stage. It targets your lightest phase within a configurable window before your alarm time, so you wake during N1 or N2 when you surface naturally refreshed, never dragged from deep sleep.
Wakes you gently, never with a jolt

Your alarm never blares suddenly. Sound begins at barely a whisper, then gently rises over the first two minutes. By five minutes it reaches full volume and stays there.\n\nIf you sleep through it, WakeIQ keeps trying; automatically switching to a different sound every two minutes so your brain gets a fresh signal. No sudden shock. Just a persistent, ever-louder nudge toward waking.
Your data stays on your phone, always

WakeIQ never collects, records, or sells any data about you. No analytics. No usage tracking. No telemetry. No silent background uploads. Everything the app knows about you (your alarms, your preferences, your sleep patterns) lives only on your device and never goes anywhere.
WakeIQ is genuinely free, open-source software released under the MIT License. There is no catch, no premium tier, and no upsell, ever. You are never expected to pay.
MIT License · free forever · no ads · no tracking · no data collection
